sourceTitle: 10 AI prompts to help marketers write better copy, faster

questions.question: When using Notion AI to draft a blog post, which part of the prompt tells the AI to follow the structure you already created?

options.text: “Draft a blog post based on [the outline above] about [topic]”
options.explanation: The phrase “based on the outline above” directs the AI to use the existing outline as the skeleton for the draft.

options.text: “Create a series of 10 LinkedIn posts”
options.explanation: That prompt is for social media, not for a blog post.

options.text: “Write a product description in less than 100 words”
options.explanation: That prompt targets product descriptions, not blog posts.

options.text: “Summarize this page and generate insights”
options.explanation: Summarizing is used for research or white papers, not for drafting a blog post.

questions.question: Which of the following is a recommended way to generate Instagram captions with Notion AI?

options.text: Explain the image or video content and state what you want the caption to convey.
options.explanation: Providing the AI with the image/video context and desired messaging lets it craft a relevant caption.

options.text: Ask the AI to write a full blog post and then copy the first paragraph as the caption.
options.explanation: Blog post content is too long and not tailored for Instagram’s short‑form style.

options.text: Use the same prompt you would use for a press release.
options.explanation: Press release prompts focus on news format, not on short, engaging social captions.

options.text: Request a list of 10 LinkedIn posts and pick one for Instagram.
options.explanation: LinkedIn posts are longer and more formal; they aren’t ideal for Instagram captions.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the “Custom AI block” in the email newsletter workflow?

options.text: To generate the entire newsletter draft from a pre‑filled outline and bullet points.
options.explanation: The Custom AI block takes the outline and bullet points and writes the full newsletter edition.

options.text: To create a database of past newsletters for reference.
options.explanation: The guide does not mention using the block for database creation.

options.text: To automatically schedule the newsletter send date.
options.explanation: Scheduling is a separate calendar function, not part of the AI block.

options.text: To design visual graphics for the newsletter.
options.explanation: The AI block focuses on text generation, not graphic design.

questions.question: If you need three different versions of a website’s hero text that are shorter, exciting, and persuasive, which prompt structure should you use?

options.text: “Create several [shorter] versions of the text below. Make it sound [exciting], [persuasive] and include the benefits of [signing up for a free trial]”.
options.explanation: This prompt explicitly asks for multiple shorter, exciting, persuasive variants while specifying the benefit to highlight.

options.text: “Write a case study about the website’s performance.”
options.explanation: A case study prompt is unrelated to website copy variations.

options.text: “Summarize this page and generate 1‑3 insights.”
options.explanation: Summarizing provides insights, not multiple copy versions.

options.text: “Draft a 500‑word blog post on the product’s benefits.”
options.explanation: A blog post is too long and not focused on the hero section.

questions.question: When turning a customer interview transcript into a case study, which element is essential to include according to the guide?

options.text: Direct quotes from the interview that illustrate the problem and solution.
options.explanation: The guide specifically says to use quotes and explain how the problem was solved.

options.text: A detailed technical specification of the product.
options.explanation: Technical specs are not required for a narrative case study.

options.text: A list of all marketing channels used in the campaign.
options.explanation: Channel lists belong to campaign reports, not case study narratives.

options.text: An exhaustive FAQ section about the product.
options.explanation: FAQs are separate content, unrelated to the storytelling focus of a case study.

questions.question: Which Notion AI feature allows you to generate insights directly from an entire database when creating a white‑paper?

options.text: AI Autofill property – Custom
options.explanation: The Custom AI Autofill property can summarize pages and extract key insights across a database, useful for white‑paper research.

options.text: Draft with AI → Blog post option
options.explanation: That option is for blog posts, not for database‑wide insight extraction.

options.text: Create a new template in the content calendar
options.explanation: Templates help structure content but don’t automatically generate insights from data.

options.text: Use the ‘Brainstorm ideas’ menu item
options.explanation: Brainstorming helps generate topics, not synthesize data from a database.

questions.question: In the ad‑copy prompt, why does the guide suggest specifying a word limit (20‑30 words) and generating multiple variations?

options.text: Short, varied copy lets you A/B test quickly and stay within platform character limits, improving conversion chances.
options.explanation: Ad platforms often restrict length; having several concise options enables rapid testing to find the most effective message.

options.text: Longer copy is always better for SEO, so the limit ensures the AI doesn’t over‑optimize.
options.explanation: Ad copy isn’t primarily for SEO; length limits are about platform constraints and readability.

options.text: The word limit forces the AI to include more product features in each variation.
options.explanation: A tighter limit actually reduces the amount of detail you can include per variation.

options.text: Generating many variations ensures the AI will automatically pick the best one for you.
options.explanation: AI provides options; the marketer must still evaluate and select the best performing copy.

questions.question: What is the benefit of using the “Brainstorm ideas” option before drafting a blog post with Notion AI?

options.text: It generates a list of potential topics, giving you a starting point for the outline and reducing writer’s block.
options.explanation: Brainstorming provides ideas that can be turned into outlines, making the drafting process smoother.

options.text: It automatically writes the full blog post without any further input.
options.explanation: Brainstorming only proposes ideas; the full post still needs to be drafted.

options.text: It formats the blog post according to SEO best practices.
options.explanation: Formatting for SEO is a later step; brainstorming focuses on idea generation.

options.text: It creates a social media calendar for the blog post.
options.explanation: Social media calendars are separate from the blog brainstorming process.

questions.question: When crafting a video tutorial script with Notion AI, why is it important to list the three features you want highlighted?

options.text: It ensures the script stays focused, covers each key point, and ties each feature to a clear benefit for the viewer.
options.explanation: Specifying features directs the AI to include them and explain their benefits, which is essential for a concise, informative tutorial.

options.text: It allows the AI to generate a script in multiple languages automatically.
options.explanation: Language generation isn’t triggered by listing features; it requires explicit language instructions.

options.text: It forces the AI to write a longer script to meet a minimum word count.
options.explanation: Listing features does not affect length; the script could be short if the features are concise.

options.text: It enables the AI to create a storyboard with visual assets.
options.explanation: Storyboarding visual assets is outside the scope of a text‑only script prompt.

questions.question: How does the guide suggest you refine the AI‑generated copy for product descriptions?

options.text: Select the best variants, ask the AI to expand or tweak them, and iterate until satisfied.
options.explanation: The workflow encourages picking favorite outputs, then refining further with additional AI prompts.

options.text: Immediately publish the first AI‑generated description without review.
options.explanation: The guide emphasizes reviewing and refining, not blind publishing.

options.text: Replace all adjectives with synonyms using the AI’s thesaurus function.
options.explanation: While synonyms can help, the guide does not prescribe a blanket synonym replacement.

options.text: Convert the description into a press release format.
options.explanation: Press releases have a different structure; product descriptions stay focused on the product itself.
